author: .paulharris
content:
I noticed `Nimbus EL` is reporting with the same client code we had expected nimbus CL to report with in graffiti watermarking, which is kind of a collision given the order in watermarks wasn't guaranteed...
We probably need to make them unique if we want the EL/CL order on watermarks to be not important, as atm in devnet6 it kind of looks like 2 CL's in the watermark `lighthouse-nimbusel-1 NB94LH0d`

author: tersec
content: sure, well, Nimbus is following https://github.com/ethereum/execution-apis/blob/v1.0.0-beta.7/src/engine/identification.md#clientcode but that list can be changed

author: .paulharris
content: I've not seen any Nimbus CL blocks including it at all, but i did notice the nimbus-el on testnet

author: tersec
content: because it's in the execution API specs

author: .paulharris
content: so the NB I can always treat as Nimbus EL?

author: .paulharris
content: NB was originally listed as the nimbus CL is all.

author: tersec
content: Well, practically right now. Currently by default Nimbus CL would use it absent any other allocation if we, e.g., merge https://github.com/status-im/nimbus-eth2/pull/8620 (which I need to look at again, it's probably alright)

author: .paulharris
content: it'd be a collsion if CL and EL are both using it... so maybe the answer is CL uses a different code...

author: tersec
content: that's one option, yes. The EL could change its code, also, either way. I do agree they should be distinct
